tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Village's Secret Knitter Revealed: 90-Year-Old 'Granny H' Found After Two Years of Spreading Joy
stories.summary: June Rose Hood, a 90-year-old grandmother, has spent two years knitting hundreds of teddy bears, lambs, and rabbits for village children, finding it the best therapy for her anxiety.

stories.headline: Cancer Survivor Trey Mancini Returns to MLB, Hits RBI Single in Stunning Comeback
stories.summary: Trey Mancini, 34, returns to MLB after nearly three years away, delivering an RBI single in his first at-bat since July 2023.

stories.headline: Serena Williams Returns to Tennis at 44, Daughters Cheering as She Rediscovers Her Serve
stories.summary: Serena Williams, 44, returns to tennis after 1,375 days away, winning her doubles match at Queen's with 18-year-old partner Victoria Mboko.

stories.headline: Fifteen Officers Paint and Build: Police Transform Deaf Teen's Room Into Safe Haven
stories.summary: Fifteen Santa Rosa police officers painted and built a new bedroom for a 14-year-old deaf girl and her family of six sharing a cramped two-bedroom apartment.

stories.headline: Container School Beneath Mumbai Flyover Gives Homeless Children Education, Meals, and Hope
stories.summary: Under a Mumbai flyover, 'Signal Shala' operates from repurposed shipping containers, educating dozens of homeless children with free schooling, meals, and robotics programs.

stories.headline: Two Fruitland Teens Rescue Elderly Neighbor Trapped Outside for 16 Hours
stories.summary: Quick-thinking young neighbors discover and free an elderly woman stuck in her yard, showing compassion and presence of mind when it mattered most.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Gharial Makes Stunning Recovery: 31 Hatchlings Released in Indian River
stories.summary: Indian conservationists celebrate as 31 gharial hatchlings are released in the Gandak River, part of the species' remarkable recovery from 13 populations in 1991 to thriving numbers today.

stories.headline: Scrap Removal Owner Donates Vehicles to Help Firefighters Train for Life-Saving Skills
stories.summary: Greg Jarvis of Greg's Scrap Removal donated more than a dozen vehicles and all towing costs to help Kawartha Lakes firefighters complete critical auto-extrication training.

stories.headline: UNC Baseball Stuns Path to College World Series After Game 1 Heartbreak
stories.summary: UNC baseball bounces back from devastating opening loss with consecutive wins and a walk-off victory to reach the College World Series for the 13th time.

stories.headline: Video Game Therapy Restores Arm Movement in Chronic Stroke Survivors
stories.summary: Chronic stroke survivors improved arm function 7.8 times better using a custom 90s-style video game with a muscle-reading device at home over six weeks.

stories.headline: Northeastern Bulrush Removed from Endangered Species List After Remarkable Population Surge
stories.summary: The northeastern bulrush rebounded from just 13 populations in 1991 to 148 across eight states, earning delisting from the Endangered Species List.

stories.headline: Single Vaccine Protects Against Both Lassa Fever and Rabies in First Human Trial
stories.summary: A breakthrough two-in-one vaccine achieves 100% protective antibody response in human trials, offering hope against two deadly diseases with one shot.
